methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Aventra is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by three former US Army Cyber officers (CEO Michael Weigand ex-Shift5, CFO Brian Retherford ex-Army Cyber with MIT Sloan MBA, CRO Jessup Meng ex-Army Cyber). The company has raised $3M to date. The relevant comp universe includes 10 private and 2 public firms in Defense & Security, though narrow peer filtering (same-stage, similar valuation tier) yields insufficient data for reliable median benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $3M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $8M. Peer-median comparison was not applicable; the broader Defense & Security sector contains 19 firms, but filtering for direct comparability produces fewer than three datapoints, making aggregate comps unreliable. Public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and top private peers (Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, Quantum-Systems) operate at substantially different scales.

The triangulated range is $6M–$9M, with a median point of $8M. This reflects limited methodological confirmation; a single applicable method constrains conviction. Key uncertainties include validation of product-market fit, revenue traction post-seed, and customer concentration risk within the defense sector. Near-term clarity on customer pipeline and repeat contract economics would materially improve valuation precision.
